content:
2 min read
Just now

--

I will say, “It depends”
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Source: https://unsplash.com/photos/monitor-screengrab-shr_Xn8S8QUWell, jokes aside, if you think the same way, you’re not entirely wrong. When all you keep hearing from your SEO team is about metrics and assurances that ‘this will work, that will work’ in about three months, and the needle is moving at a snail’s pace, it’s easy to lose faith.
That can be depressing , so let me take ownership of bringing back that faith in you (or atleast, let me try :))
SEO is not straightforward anymore. What I mean by that is it’s now about more than just driving website traffic, it’s about putting your business on every possible channel you can afford, to signal AI / latest search engines exactly what you do.
Your next question can be: Ok Ankhi I got your point, but then what about the traffic? How will I gain it back?
I’m going to tell you something you won’t like: traffic will never be the same as when traditional search was the major thing. Because people don’t want to scroll through google search results and click just to find an answer anymore.
Let me elaborate on this point a little more, because a very important concept lies here.
AI is consuming your content and everyone else’s to answer a search query, summarizing it with citations to relevant sites. While this may or may not lead to clicks, one thing is standstill: the impressions.
At this point, if they’re not clicking the source, one reason might be that AI is giving them the exact answer they wanted and they’re just not interested to looking furthur.
Well, you can’t do anything about that…but remember those impressions??
They are gold, because the next time they’re looking for that thing seriously, chances are high that they’re going to click your citation link.
And there you go, you have a click now.
So how did SEO helped here?
You wrote relevant content, got picked up by AI through techncial SEO, on-page SEO, socials, backlinks, and PR.
This means SEO has become a much larger entity than what it was before.
Therefore, in a sense traditional SEO is dead.
Whether you want to invest in SEO or not is up to you.
But remember: having only a website, not putting out blogs, case studies, or newsletter, and wishing to miraculously appear on AI search is quite impossible.
Because even beyond AI picking your site, having only a hollow website template is not going to bring in clients anyway.
So maybe next time you think SEO is DEAD, just have a bigger picture in mind about ROI.
